mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 100 basis points over 60-day holding periods in post-recession samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below zero during late-cycle expansion phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance when INDPRO trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent forces the Federal Reserve to delay easing measures, raising real discount rates and triggering immediate equity market sell-offs over 20 days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2 percent following CPI prints above 0.3 percent, or the threshold signal yields a negative information coefficient across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm genuine labor market tightening, boosting wage growth expectations and driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in over 60 percent of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates stagnating aggregate demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than half of the quarters where GDP growth stays below 1.5 percent, or the signal fails to predict return direction with accuracy above 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Falling federal funds rates alongside stable CPI inflation confirm a benign monetary easing environment, which expands valuation multiples and produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual monetary signal yields negative risk-adjusted returns during easing cycles, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility regimes.
